methodsAttempted.query: site:ufsc.br inteligencia artificial generativa politica academica UFSC
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl was not available in this isolated runtime, so discovery used public web search constrained to UFSC-controlled domains. The useful policy-specific result was the Conselho Universitario PDF for Resolucao Normativa 217/2025/CUn.
Search also returned UFSC news, course, repository, and research-showcase pages. These were not used for claims because the council resolution was the direct formal policy source.
Verified the PDF URL as an official UFSC Conselho Universitario subdomain. robots.txt on the host returned 404, so no disallow rule was discovered; the PDF responded HTTP 200.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found one direct UFSC Council normative resolution on plagiarism and academic misconduct. Claims are scoped to that PDF only: applicability of the resolution, AI-related plagiarism, AI-related academic misconduct, and investigation/detection-tool procedures.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Resolucao Normativa No. 217/2025/CUn, de 2 de dezembro de 2025
artifacts.queryUsed: site:ufsc.br inteligencia artificial generativa politica academica UFSC
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official UFSC Conselho Universitario PDF. The document states it institutes general norms for characterization, investigation, and sanction of plagiarism and academic misconduct at Universidade Federal de Santa Catarina, and includes explicit generative-AI provisions.

targets.sourceTitle: Resolucao Normativa No. 217/2025/CUn, de 2 de dezembro de 2025

stopConditions: Stop after fetching the official council resolution PDF and extracting narrowly scoped AI-related academic-integrity claims.
Do not use UFSC research, news, repository, or course pages as binding policy evidence unless they contain direct institutional policy language.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Resolucao Normativa No. 217/2025/CUn, de 2 de dezembro de 2025

artifacts.claimText: UFSC's Normative Resolution 217/2025/CUn covers plagiarism and academic misconduct in literary, technical, artistic, and scientific works produced within UFSC and applies to cases involving students, faculty, technical-administrative staff, visiting or invited researchers, scholarship holders, interns, volunteers, and others with a formal university link.
artifacts.normalizedValue: formal academic integrity resolution applies to UFSC-affiliated people and covered works

citation.citationTitle: Resolucao Normativa No. 217/2025/CUn, de 2 de dezembro de 2025
citation.publisher: Universidade Federal de Santa Catarina, Conselho Universitario
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Resolucao Normativa No. 217/2025/CUn, de 2 de dezembro de 2025
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Esta resolucao normativa tem por finalidade a caracterizacao, a definicao de niveis de ocorrencia e a orientacao quanto aos procedimentos de apuracao e de aplicacao de sancoes relacionados a plagio e ma conduta academica em obras literarias, tecnicas, artisticas e cientificas produzidas no ambito da UFSC. As normas dispostas nesta resolucao normativa aplicam-se aos casos de plagio e ma conduta academica praticados por discentes, servidores docentes e tecnico-administrativos em educacao, pesquisadores visitantes ou convidados, bolsistas, estagiarios, voluntarios e outras pessoas com vinculo formal com a Universidade.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The resolution covers characterization, occurrence levels, investigation procedures, and sanctions for plagiarism and academic misconduct in UFSC works, and applies to students, staff, researchers, scholarship holders, interns, volunteers, and others with a formal university link.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Resolucao Normativa No. 217/2025/CUn, de 2 de dezembro de 2025
citation.publisher: Universidade Federal de Santa Catarina, Conselho Universitario
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow applicability claim tied directly to Arts. 1 and 1, paragraph 1 of the official UFSC resolution.

artifacts.claimText: UFSC's resolution treats presentation of plagiarized work generated by generative artificial intelligence or equivalent technology as 'plagio por uso de inteligencia artificial' for purposes of characterization, investigation, and sanction.
artifacts.normalizedValue: plagiarized generative-AI content is an AI-use plagiarism category

citation.citationTitle: Resolucao Normativa No. 217/2025/CUn, de 2 de dezembro de 2025
citation.publisher: Universidade Federal de Santa Catarina, Conselho Universitario
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Resolucao Normativa No. 217/2025/CUn, de 2 de dezembro de 2025
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Para fins de caracterizacao, apuracao e sancao, devem ser considerados os seguintes tipos de plagio: ... VII - plagio por uso de inteligencia artificial - apresentacao de obra com conteudo plagiado, gerado por ferramenta de inteligencia artificial generativa ou tecnologia equivalente.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: For characterization, investigation, and sanction, the resolution lists AI-use plagiarism as presenting a work with plagiarized content generated by a generative AI tool or equivalent technology.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Resolucao Normativa No. 217/2025/CUn, de 2 de dezembro de 2025
citation.publisher: Universidade Federal de Santa Catarina, Conselho Universitario
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a direct restatement of the AI-use plagiarism category in Art. 4, VII.

artifacts.claimText: UFSC's resolution characterizes as academic misconduct the improper presentation of a work made wholly or partly with generative AI or equivalent technology when original authorship is required or such tools are expressly prohibited, and also characterizes nondisclosure of generative AI use as misconduct where disclosure is expressly required.
artifacts.normalizedValue: AI-generated work and nondisclosure may be misconduct in expressly defined circumstances

citation.citationTitle: Resolucao Normativa No. 217/2025/CUn, de 2 de dezembro de 2025
citation.publisher: Universidade Federal de Santa Catarina, Conselho Universitario
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Resolucao Normativa No. 217/2025/CUn, de 2 de dezembro de 2025
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: XI - apresentacao indevida de obra gerada por inteligencia artificial generativa - apresentar obra elaborada total ou parcialmente por meio de ferramenta de inteligencia artificial generativa, ou tecnologia equivalente, nos casos em que seja exigida elaboracao propria ou em que o uso dessas ferramentas seja expressamente vedado; e XII - omissao do uso de inteligencia artificial generativa - deixar de informar o uso de ferramentas de inteligencia artificial generativa, ou tecnologia equivalente, na elaboracao de obra, nos casos em que essa informacao seja expressamente exigida.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The resolution lists improper presentation of generative-AI work where original work is required or tools are expressly prohibited, and omission of generative-AI use where disclosure is expressly required.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Resolucao Normativa No. 217/2025/CUn, de 2 de dezembro de 2025
citation.publisher: Universidade Federal de Santa Catarina, Conselho Universitario
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a direct restatement of the two generative-AI misconduct categories in Art. 7, XI-XII.

artifacts.claimText: UFSC's resolution states that suspected plagiarism or academic misconduct will be investigated through inquiry or administrative process with adversarial procedure and full defense, and that UFSC must provide automated plagiarism-detection tools for the investigation commission.
artifacts.normalizedValue: suspected cases use administrative investigation with defense rights and UFSC-provided automated plagiarism detection tools

artifacts.sourceTitle: Resolucao Normativa No. 217/2025/CUn, de 2 de dezembro de 2025
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: As suspeitas de plagio e ma conduta academica serao apuradas por meio de sindicancia ou processo administrativo, nos termos desta resolucao normativa e da legislacao aplicavel, garantindo-se o contraditorio e a ampla defesa. ... A UFSC devera fornecer ferramenta(s) automatizada(s) de deteccao de plagio a serem utilizadas pela comissao de apuracao para caracterizacao e definicao do nivel de ocorrencia do plagio.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Suspected plagiarism and academic misconduct will be investigated through inquiry or administrative process with full defense rights; UFSC must provide automated plagiarism-detection tools for the investigation commission.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.title: Universidade Federal de Santa Catarina AI Academic Integrity Staged Claims
artifacts.summary: Staged claims for Universidade Federal de Santa Catarina are based on official Conselho Universitario Resolucao Normativa 217/2025/CUn. The resolution covers plagiarism and academic misconduct and includes explicit provisions on generative artificial intelligence in plagiarism and misconduct contexts.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are staged candidates only and are not canonical public policy records until promoted by the tracker review process.
This run did not identify a separate central UFSC teaching-and-learning guidance page for allowed classroom uses of generative AI; claims are limited to the council resolution.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
